days.title: Week 2, Day 1: Numbers 20-60

words.example_sentence_target: この本は二十ドルです。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kono hon wa ni-juu doru desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 三十秒待ってください。
父は四十歳です。
words.example_sentence_native: My father is forty years old.

words.example_sentence_target: 五十メートル先にあります。
一時間は六十分です。

days.cultural_note: The pattern is simple: (number 2-9) + 'juu' (ten). So 30 is 'three-ten' (san-juu).
days.pronunciation_tip: Notice the small pause between the number and 'juu', as in 'ni-juu'.

days.title: Week 2, Day 2: Numbers 70-100

words.example_sentence_target: 祖母は七十歳です。
words.example_sentence_native: My grandmother is seventy years old.

words.example_sentence_target: このテストで八十点を取った。
words.example_sentence_native: I got 80 points on this test.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kono tesuto de hachijutten o totta.

words.example_sentence_target: 九十度の角度です。
百円です。

days.cultural_note: 'Hyaku' (100) is another key building block for larger numbers.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'hy' in 'hyaku' is a quick sound, like the 'h' in 'huge'.

days.title: Week 2, Day 3: Compound Numbers
days.title_native: 第二週、三日目：複合数字

words.example_sentence_target: 二十一世紀
バスは三十五分後に来ます。
words.example_sentence_native: The bus will come in 35 minutes.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Basu wa san-juu-go-fun go ni kimasu.

words.example_sentence_target: 彼は四十八歳です。
その店には九十九種類の商品がある。
words.example_sentence_native: That store has 99 types of products.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Sono mise ni wa kyuu-juu-kyuu shurui no shouhin ga aru.

days.cultural_note: To make numbers like 21, you just say 'twenty' and then 'one': ni-juu ichi. It's very consistent.
days.pronunciation_tip: Practice saying them smoothly without a long pause: 'san-juu-go'.

days.title: Week 2, Day 4: Hundreds

words.example_sentence_target: このカメラは二百ドルでした。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kono kamera wa ni-hyaku doru deshita.

words.example_sentence_target: 三百ページの本です。
六百人が集まりました。
八百屋で野菜を買う。
words.example_sentence_native: I buy vegetables at the greengrocer.

words.example_sentence_target: 千円札

days.cultural_note: The pronunciation of 'hyaku' changes depending on the number before it (e.g., 'san-byaku', 'roppyaku'). These are sound changes you'll need to memorize.
days.pronunciation_tip: Pay close attention to the sound changes: 'hyaku' can become 'byaku' or 'ppyaku'.

days.title: Week 2, Day 5: Thousands

words.example_sentence_target: 二千年に生まれました。
words.example_sentence_native: I was born in the year 2000.

words.example_sentence_target: この自転車は三千円です。
八千メートルは高い山です。
一万円を両替してください。

days.cultural_note: Japanese counts in units of 10,000 ('man'), unlike English which uses thousands. So 10,000 is 'ichi-man', not 'juu-sen' (ten-thousand). This is a crucial concept.
days.pronunciation_tip: Like 'hyaku', 'sen' (thousand) also has sound changes. It becomes 'zen' after 'san'.

days.title: Week 2, Day 6: Reading Large Numbers
days.title_native: 第二週、六日目：大きな数字を読む

words.example_sentence_target: 二百五十グラムの肉
千九百九十年にドイツは統一された。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Sen kyuu-hyaku kyuu-juu nen ni doitsu wa touitsu sareta.

days.cultural_note: Practice breaking down large numbers into their components: thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones.
days.pronunciation_tip: Say it part by part: sen... kyuu-hyaku... kyuu-juu.

days.title: Week 2, Day 7: Reading Large Numbers 2
days.title_native: 第二週、七日目：大きな数字を読む 2

words.example_sentence_target: その車は一万千ドルです。
words.example_sentence_native: That car is eleven thousand dollars.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Sono kuruma wa ichi-man issen doru desu.

days.cultural_note: Remember the 'man' unit. 11,000 is read as 'one man, one thousand' (ichi-man issen).
days.pronunciation_tip: Notice that for 1000, we say 'sen', but when it follows 'man', we say 'issen'.
